# Explicit `HEAD` / `OPTIONS` / 405 Handling with RedwoodSDK

## Background

RedwoodSDK's router does **not** automatically map `HEAD` requests to a `GET` handler — they have to be wired up explicitly, otherwise the route returns `405 Method Not Allowed`. Build a small in‑memory file metadata API on top of RedwoodSDK that correctly handles `GET`, `HEAD`, `OPTIONS`, unknown ids, and disallowed methods, against the **real** `npm run dev` server.

## Requirements

* Use the pre-scaffolded RedwoodSDK project at the project path below (Vite plugin + `defineApp` + `rwsdk/router`).
* Implement a single endpoint `/api/files/:id` that supports `GET`, `HEAD`, and `OPTIONS`.
* Seed an in-memory file metadata store with exactly three entries (ids `f1`, `f2`, `f3` — exact name/size/contentType values given below in *Acceptance Criteria*).
* `GET /api/files/:id` returns the file metadata as JSON.
* `HEAD /api/files/:id` returns the **same** status code and response headers as the corresponding `GET`, but with an **empty body**. It must NOT return `405`.
* `OPTIONS /api/files/:id` returns `204 No Content` with the `Allow` header listing the supported methods (`GET, HEAD, OPTIONS`).
* Unknown ids (e.g. `/api/files/does-not-exist`) return `404` for `GET`, `HEAD`, and `OPTIONS`. The `HEAD` 404 response must still have an empty body.
* Any non-allowed method (`POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`) on `/api/files/:id` returns `405 Method Not Allowed` with an `Allow: GET, HEAD, OPTIONS` header.
* Implementation runs against `npm run dev` (no mocking, no production build).

## Implementation Hints

* RedwoodSDK supports per-method route objects, e.g. `route("/api/files/:id", { get: handler, head: handler, options: handler })` — see `rwsdk/router`'s method-handlers documentation.
* `HEAD` handlers must be defined explicitly; you cannot rely on `GET` being reused automatically.
* The same handler function can be reused for `GET` and `HEAD` if it builds the headers up first and only writes the body for `GET` (you can inspect `request.method`).
* For `HEAD`, the response body must be empty but `Content-Length` must equal the byte length of what `GET` would have returned for the same id.
* The automatic OPTIONS / 405 behavior of `rwsdk` may help, but you are responsible for matching the **exact** required status codes and headers described in *Acceptance Criteria* — including 404 on unknown ids for `OPTIONS`.
* Read `params.id` from the route handler argument.
* Use the standard `Response` constructor; set the `Content-Length`, `ETag`, and `Content-Type` headers manually so that `HEAD` and `GET` agree.
* Run the dev server with `npm run dev` and listen on the default Vite port `5173`.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: `/home/user/myproject`
* Start command: `npm run dev`
* Port: `5173`
* API endpoint: `/api/files/:id`
* Seed data (fixed, must use exactly these values so the verifier can derive deterministic ETag and Content-Length values):
  * `f1`: name `alpha.txt`, size `100`, contentType `text/plain`
  * `f2`: name `beta.png`, size `2048`, contentType `image/png`
  * `f3`: name `gamma.json`, size `512`, contentType `application/json`
* ETag derivation (must be deterministic and reproducible by the verifier): for each entry, `etag = 'W/"' + size + '-' + sha256(name + ':' + size).hex().slice(0, 16) + '"'`. With the seed values above this yields:
  * `f1` → `W/"100-d4639166240069c1"`
  * `f2` → `W/"2048-5899b37f0211f372"`
  * `f3` → `W/"512-3a0307a756bd191e"`
* `GET /api/files/:id` (valid id):
  * Status: `200`
  * Response headers: `Content-Type: application/json` (charset suffix allowed), `ETag` exactly equal to the derived value above, `Content-Length` equal to the byte length of the response body.
  * Body: a single JSON object with keys `id` (string), `name` (string), `size` (number), `contentType` (string), and `etag` (string). The `etag` field value must match the `ETag` response header.
* `HEAD /api/files/:id` (valid id):
  * Status: `200`
  * Response headers: identical `Content-Type`, `ETag`, and `Content-Length` as the corresponding `GET` (same id).
  * Body: empty (0 bytes).
  * Must NOT return `405` and must NOT return `404`.
* `OPTIONS /api/files/:id` (valid id):
  * Status: `204`
  * Response header `Allow` must include each of `GET`, `HEAD`, and `OPTIONS` (case-insensitive, in any order, possibly with extra methods).
  * Body: empty.
* Unknown id (`GET`/`HEAD`/`OPTIONS`):
  * Status: `404` for all three methods.
  * `HEAD` 404 must still have an empty body.
* Disallowed methods (`POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`) on `/api/files/:id` (with any id, valid or not):
  * Status: `405`
  * Response header `Allow` must include each of `GET`, `HEAD`, and `OPTIONS`.
* The dev server must not crash when receiving any of these requests, including repeated requests of the same kind.
